title: System Redundancy

content.question: What's your biggest single-point-of-failure right now?
sop: List every Critical System your business can't function without (payment processor, main client, key platform).
Count how many of them currently have a working Backup System versus none (see calculation, page 5)
Build a backup for your single highest-risk critical system this month.
Test that backup once, for real, not just in theory.
Re-audit every 6 months as your critical systems change.
questions: What's your biggest single-point-of-failure right now?
If your single most important tool, platform, or person disappeared tomorrow, what's your actual plan?
Which critical function have you never once run without its main system in place?

definitions.def: One thing whose breakage would stop everything.

content.equation_example: Backup Systems = 2, Critical Systems = 5. Stability = 2 ÷ 5 = 40% out of 100%. Higher means more of your critical functions have a working backup in place. Lower means most critical functions have zero redundancy — one failure stops you completely.
content.closing_statement: One working system is one failure away from zero.

caption:
Principle Nº 147: System Redundancy

Backup prevents collapse.

Ask yourself: What's your biggest single-point-of-failure right now?
